factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike & Inflation Fears
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025 (+23.5 bps to 4.599%), driven by renewed inflation concerns from geopolitical risks (Iran-US tensions). Higher yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JNJ, PG) and rate-sensitive financials.

factors.name: Fed Policy Transition & Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Jerome Powell exited as Fed chair amid bond market surge; Kevin Warsh taking over signals potential hawkish tilt. Traders now pricing in rate *hike* by December (per InvestingLive), reversing earlier dovish bets. Conflicting signals but upward yield trajectory hurts equity multiples.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY to 5-week highs)
factors.summary: US Dollar Index climbed to 99.30+ (5-week high) on hawkish Fed repricing and stronger US data. Dow has ~50% international revenue; strong dollar headwind translates to earnings pressure for CAT, BA, MSFT, GE.

factors.name: Energy/Commodity Inflation & Oil Surge
factors.summary: Oil prices elevated; ADNOC loading LNG tankers (supply concerns). Higher input costs for industrials (CAT, HON, MMM) and transportation (airline fuel pressures noted in news). CVX benefits but broader Dow industrials face margin compression.

factors.name: China Engagement & Trade Optimism (Partial Offset)
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded with talk of 200 Boeing jets (+potential 550 more) and 'very successful' bilateral tone. Reduces near-term tariff/trade war risk. However, few concrete deals confirmed per BBC; sentiment > substance.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US, Lebanon-Israel)
factors.summary: Lebanon-Israel tensions unresolved despite ceasefire extension; Iran ready for more talks but no major breakthrough. Uncertainty caps risk appetite, favors Treasury flows over equities. Risk premium embedded in yields.

factors.name: Berkshire Positioning Shift
factors.summary: Berkshire (major Dow holding proxy) sold $8B Chevron, bought $2.6B Delta stake, exited UNH/Visa/Mastercard. Signals selective dividend-play rotation but no broad Dow conviction signal; mixed technical message.

factors.name: IPO Pipeline Speculation & Liquidity Drain
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO filing imminent (as soon as Wed per Bloomberg); multiple mid-cap IPOs in pipeline (Cerebras, Forbright, Erock). Cramer warns of 'destructive' IPO speculation. Draws capital from established mega-caps; attention/liquidity risk.

factors.name: Weekly Decline (Friday Close -1.07%)
factors.summary: Dow fell -537 pts (-1.07%) Friday, erasing the week's gains. Nasdaq -1.54%, Russell 2000 -1.5%+. Breadth deterioration and yield-driven selloff signal weak technical footing into the weekend.

factors.name: Form 144 Insider Sales Volume
factors.summary: Multiple Form 144 filings (HF Sinclair, Ally, Equitable, etc.) suggest insider sales activity, though not unusual. No major Dow component signal, but minor negative sentiment indicator.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a classic 'real yield shock' narrative: Treasury yields surged 23.5 bps this week as inflation fears re-surfaced amid Iran-US geopolitical tensions and energy supply concerns. Fed chair transition (Powell → Warsh) and market repricing of a potential rate *hike* by December have dented equity multiples, especially dividend-heavy Dow names. The strong dollar (DXY 5-week highs) adds FX headwind for multinational earners. While Trump's China summit offered some trade-war relief (Boeing deal chatter), concrete evidence remains thin, and the bond market's decisive move lower has overwhelmed sentiment. Friday's -1.07% close erased the week's gains, signaling technical weakness and risk-off positioning into the weekend.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely next week) — critical for industrial cyclicals (CAT, MMM, HON, GE)
US PCE/CPI data (if scheduled this week/next) — will cement hawkish Fed expectations or ease them
Durable goods orders, factory orders — input demand signal for Dow industrials
Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting/guidance — tone-setting on rate path
China PMI and economic data releases — demand signal for CAT, BA, exporters
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM guidance) — validate margin resilience amid rising rates
SpaceX IPO filing + other IPO closures — capital reallocation risk
Oil price trajectory (Brent/WTI) — inflation repricing and airline/transport cost feedback
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If Fed chair Warsh signals a *dovish* pivot or cuts expectations re-emerge, or if CPI/PCE data rolls over sharply, the 10-year yield could reverse (back to 4.2–4.3%), triggering a relief rally in dividend stocks and large-cap tech. Additionally, major escalation in Iran conflict (or rapid de-escalation) could swing energy/geopolitical risk premium decisively. Positive Boeing/China trade deal *confirmation* (not just talk) could also flip sentiment bullish. Monitor Fed speakers closely for any dovish cracks in Warsh narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow has already declined -1.07% Friday; this is PHASE 2 (initial move, not yet overextended). Wait for retest of 49,700–49,800 resistance before shorting; if 10-year yields stabilize >4.50%, short risk rises. For longs, entry on a pullback to 49,200–49,300 support with confirmation that yields have peaked. Avoid chasing either direction into weekend uncertainty.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10Y yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025, climbing 23.5 bps to 4.599%, driven by inflation fears. Higher yields compress valuations for dividend-heavy Dow names (utilities, financials) and increase borrowing costs for cyclicals.

factors.name: Inflation Resurgence Narrative
factors.summary: News explicitly cites 'inflation surge' and '2022-style inflation problem bubbling back up.' Bond market rout and oil price strength (mentioned as catalyst for declines) signal investor concern over stagflationary pressures and potential Fed rate hikes rather than cuts.

factors.name: Fed Chair Transition & Rate Expectations Shift
factors.summary: Powell exits Fed as chair pro tempore (Kevin Warsh to take over). Traders now pricing next Fed move as a hike (December futures), not a cut—a sharp reversal from earlier expectations. Uncertainty around Warsh's policy stance amid high yields.

factors.name: Oil Price Strength
factors.summary: Oil cited as one of two main catalysts for Friday's broad decline (Nasdaq -1.54%, Dow -1.07%). Energy input cost inflation pressures cyclicals and transportation costs; also signals demand concerns if geopolitical premium driving moves.

factors.name: Weekly Loss Reversal
factors.summary: Friday's declines explicitly 'erase the week's gains'—suggests momentum broke mid-week. Move down already in progress with Dow -537 pts (-1.07%) on Friday, signaling Phase 2 extension rather than fresh signal.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Persistence
factors.summary: Iran tensions, Lebanon ceasefire extensions, new Ebola outbreak (80+ deaths DR Congo), Long Island Rail strike (first since 1994)—multiple supply chain and human capital shocks offset any China deal optimism from Trump summit.

factors.name: China Trade Deal Underwhelming
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded with 'few deals confirmed' per BBC; Boeing 200-jet order announced but no major trade breakthroughs. Soft landing for China growth, but lack of tariff rollback reduces upside catalyst for Dow multinational revenue (50% exposed).

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Headwinds
factors.summary: Berkshire dumped $8B Chevron; now buying Delta (risk reversal). GS downgraded by Fitch (negative outlook on credit deterioration). Limited positive guidance from cyclical heavyweights (CAT, BA, HON) in news; SpaceX IPO speculation may cannibalize investor capital.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY >99.30)
factors.summary: USD Index at fresh multi-week highs on stronger-than-expected US data. While bullish for USD-denominated balance sheets, it headwinds Dow's ~50% international revenue via FX translation drag.

factors.name: Prior Bias Continuity
factors.summary: Your last read (2026-05-16 02:46) was SELL 6/10 with no confirmation triggered. Current action (Dow -1.07% Friday, Treasury yields +23.5 bps weekly) appears to be confirmation kicking in, but move is already 4-6 hours old.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a bearish inflection driven by a sharp resurgence in inflation fears and Treasury yields, with the 10Y jumping 23.5 bps to 4.599% in a single week—the largest move since April 2025. Oil strength, Fed rate-hike expectations reversal (traders now pricing December hike), and Powell's departure amid bond volatility have broken the week's nascent rally. Geopolitical risks (Iran, Lebanon, rail strike) and Dow component credit deterioration (Fitch downgrade of GS) add downside pressure, though China deal hopes and AI enthusiasm (Cerebras IPO pop) offer only partial offsets.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (early week) — key read on industrial health and Dow composition
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production data — cyclical input for CAT, BA, GE guidance
Upcoming Dow component earnings (Q1 final reports) — UNH, MSFT, JPM, HD, V
Fed speakers / FOMC guidance (Warsh confirmation hearing risk)
PCE, PPI inflation data (if released this week/next) — will validate or quell inflation narrative
China PMI, retail sales data (Asia hours) — multinational revenue outlook
SpaceX IPO filing/announcement (reportedly Wednesday) — capital flows risk
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation would require: (1) soft inflation print (CPI/PCE cooler than expected) triggering 10Y yield collapse back below 4.3%; (2) major China trade deal or tariff rollback announcement re-igniting growth narrative; (3) Warsh dovish pivot signal or Fed hold commitment; or (4) oil price collapse on demand shock. If any of these occur, Dow could reverse sharply back above 50k within hours.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already in Phase 2 with Friday's -1.07% decline and weekly yield surge. Avoid chasing shorts now; wait for pullback retest of 49700–49900 before adding SELL positions. Consider stop/reversal if breaks above 50000 on Monday gap-up.

factors.name: Fed Leadership Transition
factors.summary: Powell appointed interim chair ahead of turbulent Warsh transition (Bloomberg, 2026-05-16T06:45). Leadership uncertainty during volatile period typically creates near-term equity headwinds as market reprices policy expectations.

factors.name: 10Y Real Yields & Inflation Concerns
factors.summary: Strong USD (DXY >99.30 per FXStreet) and sharply higher yields (cited as catalyst for Friday's -1.07% Dow decline) indicate real yields rising, pressuring dividend-heavy Dow. Energy shock concerns resurfacing (oil price strength, Iran tensions driving risk premium).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded without trade breakthroughs; Taiwan tensions unresolved ('cool it' warning); Iran war ongoing (ceasefire extended but violations continue); Long Island Rail strike halts busiest US commuter line. Multiple fault lines raising macro uncertainty.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Inflation
factors.summary: Hedge funds dumping airline stocks due to soaring fuel costs; ADNOC loading LNG tankers; energy shock lifting inflation more than GDP in Japan (ING). Input cost pressures threatening industrial earnings, especially CAT, BA, transport-linked names.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Headwinds
factors.summary: Berkshire sold $8B Chevron (energy exposure trimming); Abel betting Delta despite Buffett's airline exit signals caution; VinFast shedding $6.9B debt; no fresh positive guidance from cyclicals mentioned. Earnings quality suspect in inflation-pressure environment.

factors.name: Credit & Bond Market Stress
factors.summary: Bloomberg headline 'Global Bond Rout Catches Up With Wall Street's Risk Rally' (2026-05-15T20:30); Fitch downgraded Goldman Sachs BDC outlook to negative. Credit spreads widening into weaker equity demand.

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Your last two reads (2026-05-16 02:46 & 05:53) were SELL 6/10 and SELL 5/10 with no confirmation—this represents sustained bearish lean but lack of conviction trigger. Current -0.27% 4h move is minimal, consistent with consolidation phase.

factors.name: China Growth & Trade Uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump-Xi talks yielded 'no trade breakthroughs' despite choreography. Taiwan export strength reported (ING, 2026-05-15T21:17) but broader China PMI/GDP data absent—suggests soft near-term outlook. Tariff overhang unresolved.

factors.name: US Macro Data Lag
factors.summary: No fresh ISM, PCE, CPI, or employment data in this 24h window. Friday's decline attributed to broader yields/energy themes, not new hard data. Weekend blind-spot into Monday open.

factors.name: Positive: SpaceX & Tech IPO Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX 5-for-1 stock split approved; Cerebras blockbuster AI chip IPO (though down 10% Friday); multiple fintech/power IPOs filed (Forbright, Erock). Tech/growth optimism offset by macro headwinds; Jim Cramer concerned on IPO speculation risk.

screening.narrative: The Dow closed Friday -1.07% (49,530.94) as rising real yields (10Y spiking on Fed leadership transition anxiety and energy shock) overwhelmed late-week risk appetite. Trump's China summit failed to resolve trade/Iran tensions, leaving geopolitical risk premium embedded. Energy cost inflation re-emerging (oil strength, airline fuel headwinds) threatens industrial earnings; credit spreads widening. Your prior two SELL reads (5-6/10) reflect sustained caution but lack of confirmation catalyst. Current -0.27% 4h price action is shallow consolidation—no decisive breakout either direction—suggesting market is waiting for Monday catalysts (ISM, earnings flow, Fed policy clarity).
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (likely early week) — direct read on Dow industrial composition
Durable goods, factory orders, industrial production data — cyclical health check
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT, BA) — guidance on cost pressures & demand
Fed Chair Warsh confirmation hearings / policy signaling — rates/QT direction
China PMI/economic data — multinational earnings exposure (especially CAT, BA, materials)
Oil price action & geopolitical escalation risk (Iran, Lebanon, Taiwan) — energy input costs
10Y Treasury auction / real yield moves — valuation reset for dividend/utility Dow names
screening.risk_warning: If Fed pivots dovish (cuts signal), or China stimulus announced, or oil rolls over sharply on Iran ceasefire breakthrough, the bearish yield/real-rate thesis unwinds fast—would flip bias to BUY. Similarly, if ISM/durable goods surprise strong, near-term SELL thesis invalidated. Conversely, sustained energy shock + credit stress could accelerate decline past 49,400 support.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 consolidation with low 4h volatility. Wait for Monday ISM/earnings to trigger fresh catalyst; either short 49,800 resistance on rejection, or long 49,400 support if macro surprise bullish. Do not chase Friday's decline into weekend gap risk.

factors.name: Inflation & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Bitcoin dropped to $78k on rate hike fears; ECB signaling potential tightening (Stournaras on modest rate hike). Higher yields pressuring equities. 10-year Treasury rising, weighing on dividend-heavy Dow constituents (UNH, PG, JNJ).

factors.name: Oil & Energy Prices Rising
factors.summary: Iraq's Hormuz shipments collapsing amid conflict; oil prices rising sharply. Fuel cost inflation hitting airlines and transportation—sectors sensitive to Dow industrials (BA, CAT, UPS). Input cost squeeze ahead for earnings.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & Supply Chain Disruption
factors.summary: Iran-US tensions escalating (Trump signaling talks but 'war of choice' rhetoric); Gaza/Israel conflict ongoing; Putin-Xi meeting May 19-20 signals China-Russia alignment against US interests. Trade policy uncertainty elevated.

factors.name: China Growth & Trade Momentum
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded with 'very successful' talks but few confirmed deals. Taiwan tensions remain a flashpoint. No major China PMI or GDP releases in this window; Caterpillar and Boeing remain exposed to slowdown.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY climbed above 99.30 on stronger US economic data and rate expectations. Stronger dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue (MSFT, CAT, BA, HD, GE—translation pressure).

factors.name: Fed Policy Transition
factors.summary: Powell exits as chair pro tempore; Kevin Warsh takes over. Transition uncertainty. No immediate FOMC decision in near-term news, but rate-hold expectations persist (tighter for longer narrative).

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No major Q1 earnings releases in this 24h window. Ackman built Microsoft stake on AI/cloud thesis (bullish signal for MSFT), but broader industrial earnings cycle still ahead—CAT, BA guidance critical.

factors.name: Market Technicals & Prior Bias History
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL reads (6/10, 5/10, 5/10) since May 16 02:46 UTC with no confirmation triggered. Current -0.27% (4h) and -0.30% (24h) suggest weak continuation; move underway but shallow.

factors.name: Labor & Industrial Relations
factors.summary: Long Island Rail Road strike—first in 30+ years—signals wage pressure and potential supply chain friction. Samsung union talks ongoing; broader labor cost inflation risk for industrial Dow names.

factors.name: Credit & Liquidity Concerns
factors.summary: No major credit spread blowouts reported. Forbright IPO filing and Erock IPO suggest some continued capital market access, but broader equity sentiment weakening on yields.

screening.narrative: The Dow is under pressure from a confluence of inflationary catalysts: rising oil (Iraq supply disruptions), higher real yields, ECB tightening signals, and geopolitical escalation (Iran, Gaza, Putin-Xi alignment). The dollar's strength to 99.30+ is a headwind for multinational Dow heavyweights (MSFT, CAT, BA, GE). Trump's China summit failed to unlock trade breakthroughs, leaving tariff uncertainty intact. Three consecutive SELL reads confirm downside bias, but the move is shallow (-0.3% in 24h), suggesting this is early-stage Phase 2 with limited follow-through yet. Key risk: any dovish Fed pivot or Middle East de-escalation could flip the script quickly.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (May 22, likely)
ISM Services PMI (May 22, likely)
Durable goods orders / factory orders (May 23, likely)
CPI/PCE inflation data (May 21 or 22, exact date TBD)
Dow component earnings: CAT, BA, UNH, GS (scattered next 2 weeks)
FOMC Minutes (May 21, likely) — rate expectations signal
10-year Treasury yield moves and Fed speaker commentary
Putin-Xi summit outcome (May 19-20) — China trade/policy implications
Oil price action (Hormuz disruption ongoing)
screening.risk_warning: If inflation data comes in cooler than expected (CPI/PCE miss lower), real yields compress, and the Fed signals patience on rate cuts, equities rally sharply and this SELL thesis breaks. Also: any de-escalation in Iran-US tensions or Middle East ceasefire extension could remove geopolitical risk premium from oil and reduce yields. Conversely, if Trump pivots to aggressive tariffs on China post-summit, it could accelerate industrial weakness—confirming SELL but changing catalyst mix.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is shallow and in early Phase 2; avoid chasing shorts into the gap. Wait for retest of 49700-49800 resistance or a failed bounce from 49200 support before adding short conviction. CPI/PCE release and ISM PMI data (May 21-22) are critical near-term triggers—position sizing accordingly.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Leadership Transition
factors.summary: Jerome Powell named chair pro tempore as Kevin Warsh transition unfolds; market uncertainty over policy direction in transition period. Historical volatility around Fed leadership changes typically weighs on risk appetite.

factors.name: Inflation Concerns Resurface
factors.summary: MarketWatch and Bloomberg coverage emphasize 'inflation fears roar' and '2022-style inflation problem bubbling back up' despite no new CPI/PCE data in this window. Nominal yields elevated, real yields pressure equities.

factors.name: Trump China Summit Outcomes
factors.summary: Trump-Xi talks concluded with 'stability and stalemate' per Bloomberg; few concrete trade breakthroughs on tariffs or Taiwan. Optics positive but no de-escalation on core bilateral sticking points; uncertainty persists on trade policy direction.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran, Middle East)
factors.summary: Iran war live; Trump warns Iran 'war is worth the economic pain'; Strait of Hormuz toll threats; Lebanon ceasefire fragile. Energy supply chain anxiety and risk premium likely supporting oil but creating macro uncertainty headwind for equities.

factors.name: Dow-Weighted Names & Earnings Visibility
factors.summary: Ackman built Microsoft stake (bullish AI/cloud play), Berkshire sold $8B Chevron and added Delta ($2.8B fresh bet), Soros bought Berkshire post-Buffett exit. Mixed signals on conviction; no major Dow component earnings surprises cited in 24h window.

factors.name: Equity Weakness at Week Close
factors.summary: Stocks and bonds sold off Friday close; week's gains erased per MarketWatch. US30 down -0.30% in 24h, -0.27% in 4h—consistent minor weakness, not capitulation.

factors.name: Cass Freight & Rail Strikes
factors.summary: Long Island Rail workers strike for first time since 1994; supply chain friction. Delta Air Lines surged 64% (InvestingPro signal), but broader transportation stress evident.

factors.name: Dollar & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: DXY implications unclear from news; no explicit currency data. Dow ~50% international revenue exposure sensitive to USD moves; no catalyst mentioned to drive sharp USD appreciation or depreciation.

factors.name: ISM Manufacturing / Industrial Production
factors.summary: No new ISM PMI, durable goods, or factory output data in news window. Citi analysis notes 'broadening necessary for index upside'—implies narrowness concern, typical late-cycle signal.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield & Rate Sensitivity
factors.summary: No explicit 10Y yield quoted, but 'higher yields' mentioned in context of gold decline; suggests nominal yields firm, headwind for dividend-heavy Dow and rate-sensitive large caps.

screening.narrative: US30 closed the week with mild weakness (-0.30% YTD), erasing intraweek gains amid resurfacing inflation anxiety, Fed transition uncertainty (Powell to chair pro tempore, Warsh pending confirmation), and persistent Iran-Middle East geopolitical risk. Trump's China summit yielded 'stability and stalemate' with no major trade breakthroughs, leaving tariff direction ambiguous. Dow heavyweights show mixed conviction (Ackman bullish MSFT, Berkshire rotating into Delta, Soros entering Berkshire), but broader equity positioning appears cautious heading into uncertain macro backdrop.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation hearing / swearing-in timeline
ISM Manufacturing PMI (if released Mon-Wed)
ISM Services PMI
Durable Goods Orders data
Dow component Q1 earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA guidance)
10Y Treasury auction(s)
Iran nuclear negotiations updates
Geopolitical escalation/de-escalation on Strait of Hormuz
China economic data (industrial production, retail sales) post-Trump visit
screening.risk_warning: If Fed confirms dovish Warsh transition (lower for longer), or if Trump pivots sharply on Iran tariffs/de-escalates conflict, risk-on flows could reverse and invalidate bearish bias. Conversely, if CPI prints hot or Strait of Hormuz toll implementation shocks oil higher, downside accelerates. No major data releases in immediate window; bias hinges on sentiment and forward guidance, making it fragile to surprise.
screening.entry_guidance: Phase 2 weakness with low conviction (4/10) — avoid aggressive shorts; wait for intraday retest of 49,200–49,300 support before considering tactical entries. If 10Y breaks above 4.5% or Iran escalates, bias shifts BEARISH; if Fed chair confirmation catalyzes dovish messaging, bias flips BULLISH. Reduce size until conviction rises with hard data or clear catalyst.

factors.name: Trump China Visit & Tech Optics
factors.summary: Trump's Beijing summit with Xi generated positive optics and ceremonial pageantry; tech CEOs (Musk, Huang) accompanied him, boosting sentiment around Nvidia and Tesla. However, no major trade breakthroughs confirmed, and Taiwan arms-sales remain unresolved—mixed follow-through.

factors.name: Dow-Weighted Tech & Earnings Flow
factors.summary: Bill Ackman built Microsoft position (bullish on AI/cloud), but no fresh earnings catalysts from core Dow heavies (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT, BA) reported in past 24h. Delta Air Lines +64% on InvestingPro signal is outlier; broader composite momentum unclear.

factors.name: Energy & Oil Macro
factors.summary: UAE departed OPEC (May 1, announced May 16), signaling structural supply shifts. Iran war (day 78) creating Strait of Hormuz bottleneck risk (Iran to collect tolls); Adnoc loading LNG tankers with dark transponders. Uncertainty weighs on energy input costs and risk premium.

factors.name: Labor & Industrial Demand Headwinds
factors.summary: Long Island Rail Road first strike since 1994; Mexico teachers threatening World Cup disruption over pay; Samsung global AI boom sparking divisions and looming strike. Wage pressure and supply-chain friction building despite strong consumer spend claims.

factors.name: Consumer Spending Resilience
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Americans continuing high spending despite Iran war and inflation/gas price stress (day 78 of Iran conflict). Suggests demand holding, supporting Dow consumer/cyclical names (UNH, HD, V).

factors.name: Small-Cap Underperformance
factors.summary: Russell 2000 led US stocks lower on Friday; small caps showing 'latest setback' with 'more trouble could lie ahead' per MarketWatch. Signals broadening weakness and concentration risk in large-cap Dow names—less healthy tape.

factors.name: Rate Expectations & Yields
factors.summary: Powell exits Fed after 'one of the wildest eras in history'; ECB considering modest rate hike (Stournaras comment). No fresh US CPI/PCE data in window. 10-year yield direction unclear; rate-sensitive Dow components (JPM, GS, financials) in flux.

factors.name: International Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Israel-Hamas ceasefire fraying (strikes continue on Lebanon despite extension); Ebola outbreak (80+ deaths, no vaccine); ISIS leader killed but regional instability rising. Adds tail risk to multinational revenue (Dow ~50% international).

factors.name: Recent Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Dow down -0.27% in 4h, -0.30% in 24h; prior bias calls (May 16, 08:02 & 10:14) were SELL5/10 with no confirmation, then shifted to NEUTRAL4/10 at 11:49. Weakness persisting despite positive China optics suggests sellers holding.

factors.name: Citi 'Broadening Necessary' Call
factors.summary: Citi notes broadening is 'a necessary condition for meaningful index upside from here,' implying current rally lacks breadth. South Korea's KOSPI flagged for caution, suggesting Asia tech/semiconductor demand softening.

screening.narrative: US30 is caught between conflicting forces: Trump's Beijing visit and tech-CEO optics provided a temporary catalyst for sentiment, but no material trade deals materialized, Taiwan remains a sticking point, and the Iran war—now day 78—is creating structural energy and supply-chain friction. Domestically, labor disputes (LIRR, Mexico teachers, Samsung) and small-cap weakness suggest the market is broadening downward, a red flag for large-cap concentration. Consumer spending claims remain resilient, but input-cost pressures (energy, wage inflation) and geopolitical tail risks (Israel-Lebanon, Ebola) are mounting. The Dow's -0.30% overnight decline despite positive optics suggests sellers are not convinced, and the prior two SELL signals (early May 16) have shifted to NEUTRAL—indicating either capitulation or a lack of conviction on either side.
catalysts_ahead: US ISM Manufacturing PMI (mid-week expected) — critical read on Dow's industrial backbone
US ISM Services PMI — breadth indicator for cyclical services exposure
Durable goods orders & factory orders — forward-looking industrial capex demand
Dow component earnings (JPM, GS, UNH, CAT, BA, HON, GE guidance) — valuation + cyclical confidence
China PMI data release (if scheduled) — demand signal for Caterpillar, Boeing, materials
ECB meeting or rate-guidance update — yield implications for multinational earnings translation
Iran nuclear talks progress or Strait of Hormuz developments — energy cost shock risk
Fed speaker commentary (post-Powell transition period) — rate path uncertainty
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) China PMI or US ISM manufacturing rebounds sharply, signaling industrial demand revived; (2) Iran war de-escalates and oil rallies, easing input costs; (3) Dow breadth recovery confirmed (Russell 2000 outperformance, financials & industrials leadership re-established); (4) No new geopolitical shocks (Middle East escalation, Israel-Lebanon flare-up) and corporate guidance remains upbeat. Conversely, a sharper small-cap washout, energy supply shock, or earnings miss from Dow heavies (JPM, CAT, BA) could accelerate selling.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway but weak (only -0.30% in 24h after China bounce). Wait for retest of 49,500–49,600 resistance before committing to further shorts; if that level breaks with volume, reassess bias. For longs, wait for clear break above 49,750 with small-cap co-confirmation before entering (lack of breadth is the core concern).
